name: bpftrace (High-Level Tracing Language for Linux eBPF)

performance.notes: Kernel-level eBPF programs; minimal overhead on traced path

agent_readiness.notes: High-level language for Linux eBPF kernel tracing. bpftrace -e 'tracepoint:syscalls:sys_enter_openat { printf("%s\n", comm); }'. Probes: kprobe, tracepoint, uprobe, USDT, profile. bpftrace -l to list available probes. One-liners for disk I/O, network, syscalls. opensnoop.bt, execsnoop.bt, tcpconnect.bt. Brendan Gregg's tools at github.com/brendangregg/bcc. Production-safe kernel observability. Linux 4.9+.

gotchas: Kernel version 4.9+ required; some features need 5.x+
best_when: Production Linux performance debugging with minimal overhead
Syscall tracing and profiling on production servers
Understanding kernel behavior without modifying code
Custom performance tools when perf/strace insufficient
summary: bpftrace is the high-level scripting language for Linux eBPF kernel tracing. One-liners for syscalls, disk I/O, network, CPU profiling. tracepoint/kprobe/uprobe probes. Production-safe with minimal overhead. bpftrace -e 'probe { action }'. Part of Brendan Gregg's performance toolkit. Linux 4.9+, root required. Essential for production performance debugging on Linux.
